Fix toolbar About button not appearing

text label. "About" was only passed as the tooltip so the button

commit.message:
Refactor: inline reading pane replaces standalone entry page (#108)

* Eliminate standalone entry page, show full detail in reading pane

Replace the two-step workflow (list → preview → "Open full page") with
inline entry detail in the reading pane. All actions (Star, Mark Unread,
Fetch Full Content, Summarize, Save, View Original) are now available
directly without navigating away.

- Upgrade _loadInReadingPane() with complete entry detail and action buttons
- Add entry-mode keyboard shortcuts (j/k scroll, s/u/v/f/b/z actions, q/Esc back)
- Sync browser URL via history.pushState; handle popstate for back/forward
- Support ?entry= query param for direct entry loading from redirects
- Convert /entries/{id} from template render to redirect to list page
- Pass has-save-services/has-kagi-configured config to all entry list templates
- Delete templates/entry.html (no longer needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix multiple UI bugs in reading pane

- Fix keyboard duplicate listeners by only binding once
- Fix mark unread affecting multiple entries (single listener pattern)
- Fix entry skipping in unread mode by deferring removal during entry mode
- Fix n/N next unread detection in entry mode
- Remap n/p to next/prev entry, N/P to next/prev unread
- Delegate unhandled keys in entry mode to page-specific handlers (1/2/3/4)
- Fix flash messages: solid backgrounds, fixed position, dark mode support

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix mobile hamburger overlapping header content

- Increase padding-top on mobile for header/content/entry-detail areas
- Adjust hamburger button position for better spacing
- Hide hamburger button when sidebar is open to prevent overlap

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix mobile overflow issues across all pages

- Add overflow-x: hidden on body to prevent horizontal scroll
- Add word-break/overflow-wrap on code, page-content, reading-pane,
  entry-content, and content-snippet for long text
- Make article/entry tables horizontally scrollable (display: block)
- Make tab-bar horizontally scrollable with hidden scrollbar on mobile
- Responsive settings-th width, flash container, and dialog on mobile
- Page-content tables: horizontal scroll on small screens

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix cargo fmt and clippy warnings

- Format pages.rs with cargo fmt
- Remove redundant "unread" variant in wildcard match arm

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add smooth island mask so terrain fades naturally to water at edges

Radial mask starts fading at 70% of grid radius and blends terrain
height toward sea level by 95%, avoiding any hard rectangular cut at
the grid boundary.

Fix Tailwind CSS v4 build: replace var() refs with oklch values in @theme

The @theme inline block was using var() references to :root custom properties,
which triggers an "Invalid code point" bug in Tailwind CSS v4's variable hashing.
Replaced with direct oklch color values matching the original hex palette.

Add GitHub Actions workflow for automated exe build

- Triggers on push to master, PRs, version tags (v*.*.*), and manually
- Runs on windows-latest with Python 3.11
- Installs deps + pyinstaller, builds with GoonZuFarm.spec
- Uploads exe as artifact (14-day retention) on every build
- Creates a GitHub Release with the exe automatically on version tags
  (e.g. git tag v1.0.0 && git push --tags)

Planning: larger Combined Net Worth label + summary header font sizes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix portal submissions not appearing in Returns table

Two bugs fixed:
1. shopifyReturnId was hardcoded to 'mock-new' causing unique
   constraint violation on 2nd+ submissions (silent fail, no record)
2. When shop param was empty, records saved under 'mock-shop'
   instead of the actual store domain

Now uses unique timestamp-based returnId and resolves shop
from the Session table as fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Comprehensive security hardening and stability improvements

- Fix path traversal vulnerability using path.relative instead of startsWith
- Add incremental stdin size limit (1MB) to prevent buffer DoS
- Add JSON input structure validation in on-edit hook
- Wrap all CLI command handlers in try-catch error boundaries
- Replace busy-wait sleepSync with Atomics.wait (non-CPU-spinning)
- Add PID+timestamp to lock files to detect recycled PIDs (120s stale threshold)
- Validate lock file JSON structure before use
- Unify daemon lock mechanism (mkdir-based, removed dual lock/pid inconsistency)
- Add top-level try-catch in runDaemon with proper lock cleanup on fatal error
- Daemon graceful shutdown now waits for in-flight sync (max 10s)
- Daemon auto-shuts down when all sessions are removed
- Config cache now invalidates on file mtime change and resets on file deletion
- Config values validated (positive, finite numbers only)
- Fix readStdin memory leak by removing event listeners after use
- Normalize file paths to relative in addFileChange, reject escaping paths
- Cleanup command re-checks worker status before deletion (TOCTOU mitigation)
- Simplify sanitizeId with direct regex + max 128 char length limit
- Improve stash pop failure logging with recovery instructions
- Hook backup handles copyFileSync failure gracefully

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Track AB: ALiBi (Attention with Linear Biases) kernel support

Metal STEEL forward kernel:
- New buffer(9) parameter `alibi_slopes` [H] in generate_steel_forward_source()
- ALiBi bias injected after softcap: slope * log2e * (k_pos - q_pos) in log2 domain
- Same injection in generate_flash_decode_partial_source() at buffer(6)
- KernelKey has_alibi field already added in prior commit (softcap track)

C++ primitive (mfa_attention.cpp):
- MFAttention::eval_gpu STEEL path: enc.set_input_array(inputs[alibi_idx], 9)
- Flash Decode partial path: enc.set_input_array(inputs[3], 6)
- mfa_attention_alibi_forward() free function: inputs=[q,k,v,alibi_slopes]
- Fallback for f32 (no STEEL ALiBi kernel): routed to _alibi_sdpa_ref in Python

Python (attention.py):
- flash_attention(..., alibi_slopes=None): new public parameter
- _alibi_sdpa_ref(): pure-MLX reference, backward oracle for vjp
- _make_mfa_alibi_custom(scale, causal): custom-vjp wrapping mfa_attention_alibi_forward
  - gradient for alibi_slopes returned as zeros (slopes not trainable)
- _mfa_alibi_forward(): dispatch path with contiguity enforcement

nanobind (bindings.cpp):
- mfa_attention_alibi_forward binding added

Tests (4 new, 184 total):
- TestALiBi: zero_slopes_is_noop, slopes_reduce_distant_scores,
  matches_reference (N=48, moderate slopes), falcon_slopes_causal

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Creative pipeline v13: image classification, phone/rating extraction, style differentiation, prompt caching

Phase 1 overhaul addressing totalcomfort.biz and autobodypdrcrew.com rendering failures:

- Image type classification (photo/icon/illustration/decorative) filters SVG service
  icons from siteImageUrls so agents get real photos, not cartoon icons
- Phone number extraction (tel: links, header/footer text scan, body fallback)
- Aggregate rating extraction (JSON-LD, microdata, page text patterns)
- Style reinforcement: prepend style-specific reminders to each creative agent's
  user message so style identity survives the massive JSON payload
- Third-party brand penalty (-40) prevents Google/Facebook/Yelp logos from being
  selected as the business logo; carousel penalty (-15) for partner logo sliders
- Open-ended industry detection replaces hardcoded 20-industry preset list
- Temperature gaps widened (Modern 0.5, Classy 0.4, Unique 1.0) for differentiation
- Anthropic prompt caching (cache_control: ephemeral) on all 6 agent system prompts
- ScreenshotOne ad/cookie banner blocking for cleaner vision analysis screenshots
- Auto-seed agent skills on build (version-gated, safe to run every deploy)
- v13 creative prompts add PHONE NUMBER and RATING/REVIEWS sections
- Remove 4 superseded planning docs; keep MODEL-ANALYSIS.md and AGENT-ENHANCEMENT-IDEAS.md

commit.message:
fix: generate correct OECD Data Explorer URLs

SDMX dataflow IDs are compound ("DSD_REG_ECO@DF_GDP"); the Data Explorer
requires only the part after "@" in df[id], not the full compound ID.

- Adapter: inject _dataset_url into each parsed dataflow using the correct
  Data Explorer format (df[id]=DF_GDP, df[ag]=agencyID)
- Frontend: fix inferDatasetUrl fallback — strip DSD prefix from compound
  IDs and point to data-explorer.oecd.org instead of legacy stats.oecd.org

commit.message:
Add quote cache to Finnhub service for faster watchlist switching

Finnhub free tier requires 1 API call per ticker (no batch endpoint).
With 37+ tickers across QuoteMonitor + Heatmap polling every 15s, the
60 calls/min rate limit caused 30+ second delays when switching
watchlists.

Fix: 12-second in-memory cache in FinnhubService.getQuotes() so
overlapping tickers between panels hit cache. Batches of 10 concurrent
requests for uncached symbols. Switching watchlists now takes <500ms.

commit.message:
docs: add deployment guide for Windows, Ubuntu, and AWS

Covers three platforms with step-by-step instructions:
- Windows with Docker Desktop
- Ubuntu server with Docker Engine
- AWS with three tiers: single EC2, EC2 + RDS/ElastiCache, ECS Fargate
Also includes production configuration, SSL/TLS setup with Caddy,
backup strategies, and troubleshooting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: switch npm publish from OIDC to NPM_TOKEN, remove spurious code-change guard

OIDC Trusted Publisher was not configured on npmjs.org, causing ENEEDAUTH
on every publish attempt. Switch to NODE_AUTH_TOKEN via NPM_TOKEN secret
and add registry-url to setup-node so npm picks up the .npmrc auth.

Also remove the 'no code changes → skip' guard: a deliberate version bump
commit should always trigger publish if the version is new on npm.

commit.message:
Add notebook 44: Gold stop dwell inferred from VP trajectory

Infers actual stop dwell using vehicle position + speed instead of
GTFS-RT predictions. Detects stationary vehicles (speed <= 1 m/s)
within 40m of a scheduled stop via haversine. Each 30s VP bucket
near a stop = 30s of inferred dwell. Accuracy ±30 seconds.

Also adds to pipeline_config:
- GOLD_STOP_DWELL_INFERRED table constant
- VP_STOP_RADIUS_METERS = 40
- VP_STATIONARY_THRESHOLD_MPS = 1.0

Quality report includes Route 29 / 35th Ave targeted check.

commit.message:
falco: add falco-bpf.service drop-in for probe install and logging

The upstream falco-bpf.service unit has two problems:
  1. No ExecStartPre — the eBPF probe is never downloaded, so Falco
     fails immediately on first boot with a silent scap_init error.
  2. StandardOutput=null — this also silences StandardError, hiding
     all Falco output from journalctl.

Add a systemd drop-in (falco-bpf.service.d/10-atomic.conf) that:
  - Runs 'falcoctl driver install --type ebpf' before Falco starts,
    downloading the kernel-specific probe on first boot and after
    kernel upgrades automatically.
  - Overrides ExecStart to pass -c /etc/falco/falco.yaml explicitly.
  - Restores StandardOutput=journal / StandardError=journal so Falco
    alerts and errors appear in the journal.

Wire the drop-in into the agentic-os Containerfile via COPY so it is
baked into every image build.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(code-similarity): add clone detection module

Detects duplicated code across source files. Tokenizes via tree-sitter
(Rust and TypeScript), normalizes identifiers and literals so structurally
identical code matches regardless of naming, then finds repeated token
sequences using suffix arrays. Reports clone groups with 1-indexed source
locations.

commit.message:
fix: command/shell throws on non-zero exit codes (#615)

## Summary

Fixes #614 — workflows reported "succeeded" even when shell commands
failed (e.g., `nvidia-smi: command not found`).

### Problem

`command/shell` captured exit codes as data but never threw on non-zero
values. The workflow engine determines step success by whether the
method throws, so every shell step was treated as successful regardless
of the command's actual outcome.

### Why this approach

The simplest correct fix is to make `command/shell` throw before writing
any data when the exit code is non-zero:

- **Throw before data writes** — failed commands should not persist
incorrect or misleading result data. If the command failed, there's
nothing useful to store as a "result".
- **No engine changes needed** — the existing workflow engine try/catch
chains already handle `stepRun.fail()`, `allowFailure`, and error
logging. Making the model throw is the standard convention for signaling
failure.
- **`ignoreExitCode` opt-out** — an optional `ignoreExitCode: true`
attribute is available for definitions where non-zero exits are expected
and the caller wants data persisted regardless.

### Changes

- **`src/domain/models/command/shell/shell_model.ts`** — Added
`ignoreExitCode` (optional boolean) to `ShellInputAttributesSchema`.
Throws `Error` before `writeResource`/`createFileWriter` when exit code
is non-zero and `ignoreExitCode` is not set.
- **`src/domain/models/command/shell/shell_model_test.ts`** — Updated 4
tests to expect `assertRejects`, added 3 new tests (ignoreExitCode
suppression, exit 0 normal return, no data on failure).
- **`integration/keeb_shell_model_test.ts`** — Updated failing-command
test to expect CLI exit code 1.
- **`integration/workflow_new_architecture_test.ts`** — Fixed 15 model
definitions that used invalid shell commands (bare strings like `"Hello
from model"` instead of `"echo 'Hello from model'"`). These were
silently failing before but passing because exit codes were swallowed.
- **`integration/input_override_validation_test.ts`** — Fixed CEL
expression test input to be a valid shell command.
- **Skill docs** (`SKILL.md`, `references/api.md`,
`references/examples.md`) — Added error handling guidance establishing
the convention: check for failure first, throw before writing data, only
persist data on success.

### User impact

- **Breaking change for workflows using `command/shell` with commands
that exit non-zero.** These workflows will now correctly report as
failed instead of falsely succeeding.
- **Opt-out available:** Add `ignoreExitCode: true` to any shell model
definition that intentionally uses commands with non-zero exit codes.
- **`allowFailure: true`** on workflow steps continues to work — the
engine catches the exception and allows the workflow to proceed.

## Test Plan

- [x] `deno check` — passes
- [x] `deno lint` — passes
- [x] `deno fmt` — passes
- [x] `deno run test` — 2692 passed, 0 failed
- [x] `deno run compile` — binary compiles

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

pipeline TTS with LLM streaming to reduce voice response latency

TTS synthesis now starts as soon as the first sentence boundary is
detected during LLM token streaming, rather than waiting for the full
response. A concurrent player loop prefetches the next sentence while
the current one plays, eliminating inter-sentence gaps. Playback is
routed through AudioContext (instead of HTMLAudioElement) to share the
warm audio device and reduce first-syllable clipping.
